name: Overjustification Effect — Reward Undermines Intrinsic Motivation
description: When an external reward is introduced for an activity someone already enjoys, their intrinsic motivation decreases. Removing the reward later leaves them less motivated than before.

history: loves drawing, does it every free period voluntarily

stimulus.description: Teacher announces: 'If you draw a picture, you'll get a gold star certificate!' Child draws, receives certificate. Two weeks later, certificates stop. Free drawing time available.

expected.behavior: draws less during free time than before the reward was introduced

sources: Lepper, Greene & Nisbett 1973 — Undermining Children's Intrinsic Interest
Deci & Ryan 1985 — Intrinsic Motivation and Self-Determination
